summary: Iteration 7 frontend tests passed. Diary tab, 20 mood emojis, add/remove/persist via localStorage 'scheduler.diary.v1', and Day Mode toggle (body.day-mode + 'lumora.dayMode'=1) all verified end-to-end.
passed: Bottom nav renders all 4 buttons: nav-tasks-btn, nav-shopping-btn, nav-metime-btn, nav-diary-btn
nav-diary-btn navigates to diary-page with 'Journal' heading and diary-empty initial state
Diary page renders 20 mood buttons (aria-label 'Mood ...'); testid 'diary-mood-🥳-btn' clickable; selected mood transform scale(1.1) applied
diary-text-input + diary-add-btn creates diary-entry-{id} card with mood emoji + text + formatted timestamp (e.g., 'Jun 26, 6:37 PM')
Entry persists across full page reload (localStorage 'scheduler.diary.v1' contains entry JSON)
diary-remove-{id} removes entry and restores diary-empty state
day-mode-toggle in tasks header toggles body className to 'day-mode' and persists localStorage 'lumora.dayMode'='1'; toggling back clears it
Tasks view still renders post-toggle (My Day header, stats Total/Done/Active, Smart Suggestions, Share streak, task list) — no regression
notes: Emoji-in-testid worked fine without falling back to aria-label. Day mode visual palette change confirmed via body class + localStorage; CSS rules under body.day-mode will apply (not pixel-validated).
